item.title: Saw this elsewhere on here. It's a warning that needs to be spread far and wide. Don't use these free resource websites
item.dc:creator: SandettieLightVesselAutomatic

item.title: Christopher Brown (trigger warning)
item.description: Christopher Brown I'm breaking the silence around my brother's abuse and suicide. My parents sent my brother away at the age of 14. He was sent to a school where the boys all lived together, and he wasn't allowed to be with his family. As an 8 year old boy, this was devastating to me. My brother was my hero. They then sent him to an abuse factory called Robert Land Academy, a military style academy where you're beaten and broken. He ran away from there 3 times (one time when he came home on leave), the third time, he never came back. After his first runaway attempt, my parents had him arrested and charged (he took 500$ from them), they told this 15 year old boy he didn't have a home anymore and he couldn't come back. He then ran away, laid down side by side with another boy on train tracks, and was run over by the train. My parents told their children and others. "He fell asleep on the tracks, and he never woke up." The fact is, he and the other boy could have laid one meter away, off of the tracks, in complete safety instead of across the tracks in guaranteed danger. He was only 16 when he died, and I was 10 years old. The police told my family it was a suicide, and my parents begged/persuaded the coroner to change the cause of death. They also threatened the media and police with legal action. Preserving my parents' image was more important than having my brothers voice and suffering heard. My parents are Catholic and wanted a Catholic funeral, which you can't have for a suicide. It's the ultimate mortal sin. The statement was changed to accidental death, and he had his Catholic funeral . My parents never took accountability for their negligence and cruelty. Fast forward to 13 year old me, I couldn't focus in school between the loss of my brother, my parents psychotic religious abuse, and the horrible reality of my life. My parents' response to me having a hard time in school was to send me to the same military school, RLA. I can't begin to describe the horrible mental state I was in there, the constant reminders of my family throwing their kids away and what my brother endured before he died. My family told me I'm not allowed to talk about him, they've threatened me, bribed me, told me they'll never talk to me and have disrespected the pain they caused him. I've felt his voice and his soul screaming inside of me as of recent, telling me to look for him in some way. I found a video on April 4th that someone made for him. I have no idea who made it, but I'm happy the truth is finally out there. https://www.reddit.com/r/troubledteens/comments/1h476or/escaping_robert_land_academy_the_final_moments_of/?rdt=47911
item.dc:creator: weareheretodrinkyourbeer

item.title: Cybertruck hunters
item.description: While trying to pull myself out of a depression induced disassoiative bullshit storm I came here to see funny things. Saw this somewhere in another thread in a reply today. I had to post it. It's awesome. ANY form of resistance, even this kind of stuff is good. Also fuck cyber cucks and fuck elmo muskrat and tronald dump. Mute or unmute. It's just classical music that fits whats going on in the video. Stay safe friends. Shits getting scary..er

item.title: A tools dump. For us engineers and technicians.
item.description:
An offshore oil rig emergency escape. Impressive how things have finally evolved the right direction.
2 :: Hauling a substation transformer. Sorry for the stupid audio.
Ceiling work made easy. Once again guys, this IS NOT AN AD. I'm only throwing ideas, and let's find out how to use them without spending precious dollars.
4 :: Drill attachment for efficient wiring. Well, this is cheap and useful when you have to do a LOT of connections.
A Nice Lighter.
6 :: Plowing a huge field with a (probably GPS assisted) tractor. Just because beautiful images.
Removal of excess cardboard edge. NOT a tool for home use !
8 :: Simple invention DIY Handmade Tools. Yes this is my point. You can get that electric machine in second hand for somle five bucks. The rest is UP TO YOU.
This Screwdriver. Looks more like an existing tool, but will be handy when you have days of work to do.
10 :: How books are printed. I studied typography when I was 15, .... things have changed a lot.
Forging a big iron nail. AAAfrika system. Best nails you can get.
12 :: Case Steam Traction Engine Plowing. OOOCH that is real power.
Get a good knocker. Avoid Yehowah and other stupid brain thieves.
14 :: Nice and smooth techniques that doesn't eat your savings.
Physical Key Copying. Mostly to remember to never lend your keys....
16 :: TIL about plastic welders. We all once needed that tool.
We humans are evolving. Once again I show an idea, not an ad.
18 :: Casting low-melt alloy bars. It's just beautiful.
Car Factory Robots. Smart. Economical.
20 :: Broom head making. We need more of these than cars and there are no robots.
Nice tool. This is not an ad, just an idea !
item.dc:creator: TerribleBot

item.title: Dumps of Unusual Size 41: Science, Part Two

item.dc:creator: Schadwen

item.title: Before the foods comes the snuggles
item.description: It’s time for me to leave the kitten room, which means it’s time for mama to get her nighttime wet food. It serves two purposes - one, to give her the nutrients to continue nursing her five brand new babies, of course. And two, it allows me enough time to leave the kitten room without her following me out and chasing down Madison and Scooty Booty who she smells and senses on the other side of the kitten room door. The tension between these three is PALPABLE. There’s only so much I can do to keep Scoots and Mads from the kitten room door. They’re curious, and they remember their time in the kitten room where they were born four years ago. I’ve had a total of TWO kitten foster fails over 11 years of fostering and these two won’t let me forget it. In any event, I’m headed out.
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: Not exactly a book, but it allowed us to read many
item.description:
In my previous post I mentioned that I have been able to travel because of my reading habit (in a round-about way). Here are a couple book related things I've been able to see because of my reading habit. I was able to travel to London in 2023 and spend a day in the British Museum. The Rosetta Stone made me feel things that I never expected. I actually got choked up seeing this and knowing what it represented. It's an amazing sight.
I was able to visit with a friend from Nigeria, so we had to see the Benin Bronzes. These really need to be returned but I'm glad we were able to see them and he was able to share the importance of them to me.
I missed Ea-Nasir, but I was able to see some other cuneiform writing. It's MUCH smaller than I was expecting. Each stroke is only ~1/8 inch (~3mm). I'm not sure what I was expecting, but it wasn't that.
Some Egyptian hieroglyphics.
In May 2024, I went to Austin, Texas and was able to see one of the few remaining complete copies of the Gutenberg Bible, printed in 1450s. Amazing works of art, regardless of what you believe. https://www.hrc.utexas.edu/gutenberg-bible/
item.dc:creator: Dazrin

item.title: Everyone should read at least one Discworld novel
item.description: (these are the only Discworld books in English I own) Sir Terry was (and still is) my favourite author. I was introduced to his works by my cousin who gave me a polish translation of "Guards! Guards!"... a quarter of century ago.

item.title: That moment you know you don’t completely suck as a single parent
item.description: My oldest daughter is 22 and is heading to Ireland in a few months for her Master’s degree!! She is working at Walmart until then. I’m incredibly proud of her and how she handled this situation. Although, it sucks I wasn’t there to shield her.
item.dc:creator: ifinallydiditnumber1345678

item.title: What Happened Today #67
item.description: They’re not just pushing the limits — they’re detonating them. Trump’s machine is firing, gutting, freezing, and buying out every lever of public service that still protects people instead of punishing them. Today’s stories aren’t isolated; they’re coordinated strikes on independence, legality, and basic decency. From throwing refugees and retirees under the bus to breaking websites, defunding school lunches, and building billion-dollar detention networks, the strategy is clear: consolidate power, eliminate resistance, and make cruelty look like policy. Don’t normalize this. Fight for facts. Fight for function. Fight back. Subscribe free at https://govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/2025/04/07/supreme-court-trump-deportations-alien-enemies-act/82758348007/ 2. https://www.axios.com/2025/04/07/supreme-court-wrongfully-deported-el-salvador 3. https://www.nbcnews.com/news/world/china-vows-fight-end-trump-raises-tariffs-104-rcna200138 4. https://abcnews.go.com/US/wireStory/trump-administration-fires-senior-navy-female-officer-nato-120579606 5. https://apnews.com/article/weather-spanish-translation-noaa-nws-trump-71c727dbe2e4956247c9d9152494f1dc 6. https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/trump-administration-terminates-some-usaid-contracts-providing-lifesaving-food-aid 7. https://www.yahoo.com/news/us-justice-dept-mobilized-armed-150244604.html? 8. https://apnews.com/article/state-department-trump-foreign-service-diplomacy-719841ea8d0fc9f75ae41e393dc0f845 9. https://www.newsweek.com/trump-confirms-iran-talks-saturday-warns-great-danger-if-they-fail-2056532 10. https://www.nbcwashington.com/news/president-trump-politics/trump-administration-president-military-parade/3886209/ 11. https://www.texastribune.org/2025/04/07/greg-abbott-special-election-sylvester-turner/ 12. https://www.nbcnews.com/health/health-news/rfk-jr-says-plans-tell-cdc-stop-recommending-fluoride-drinking-water-rcna200127 13. https://federalnewsnetwork.com/workforce/2025/04/appeals-court-restores-doge-access-to-sensitive-information-at-us-agencies/ 14. https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/us/after-trump-funding-cuts-us-catholic-church-ends-resettlement-work/ar-AA1Ctzgu 15. https://thehill.com/regulation/court-battles/5235598-trump-firings-court-ruling/ 16. https://gizmodo.com/social-security-website-crashes-blamed-on-doge-software-update-2000586092 17. https://www.nytimes.com/2025/04/07/us/politics/trump-administration-immigrant-detention-facilities-services.html 18. https://www.politico.com/news/2025/04/07/noem-offers-buyouts-to-dhs-workers-00277571 19. https://www.bangordailynews.com/2025/04/07/politics/state-politics/maine-aaron-frey-lawsuit-usda-brooke-rollins-frozen-funds-school-nutrition-children-students/

item.title: Phone-clearing dump 289
item.description:
Third, fourth, umm, look. How about I just give you my card?
………………………. ………………………. ………………………. ………………………. ………………………. ………………………. ………………………. ………………………. Warning, next image contains a sex scene that can be triggering ………………………. ………………………. ………………………. ………………………. ………………………. ………………………. ………………………. ……………………….
Oh. Yeah, think I saved this out of fascination as they….well. You have the title, see summary.
Apparently I needed to confirm lunatics like this exist
I don’t know who this is tbh
I’m shook
Die without using it a single time?! Sure!

item.title: This is weeks old and I just learnt about it. I feel like I'm going to vomit.
item.description:
https://www.cbsnews.com/news/man-captive-20-years-fire-escape-stepmother-kimberly-sullivan/
Fuck
https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/biological-mother-connecticut-man-allegedly-held-captive-stepmom-decad-rcna196853 Imagine being this POS lawyer and defending this kind of blatant horror. "Innocent until proven guilty" herpaderpderpfuckoff Whatever. I'm checking out. Edit: For the lot of you assuming I don't believe in due process, you're wrong. (LOL@some of the comments I happened to skim over.) Will I elaborate on it? No. Also, I realise this is just one in the endless cases like these. It's especially unnerving when it's closer to home.
item.dc:creator: unwaver

item.dc:creator: thewillofsaga

item.title: First (Stolen) ADHD memedump
item.description:
Hey y'all! Long time lurker, first time poster.
Today, at 36, I was diagnosed with ADHD combined presentation
Decided what better way to...celebrate than giving back!
After years of thinking my symptoms were flaws, I felt vindicated.
I was a "gifted" kid growing up and the oldest of 4.
So, it was easy to miss the signs in my adolescence and teen years.
But in my late twenties, I felt that these "flaws" were actually signs of something bigger.
Got laid off in November of '23, which led to me getting Medicaid.
So, I had the health insurance to see what was up.
I've been in therapy and on medication for over a year for my initial diagnosis.
Which was general anxiety and depression from trauma.
These two tools have been invaluable in my journey.
But, something still felt...off.
I have recently switched therapists, my first one retired.
So my new therapist finished her diagnosis today.
Turns out I have ADHD, combined presentation.
Meaning I'm inattentive and hyperactive, which checks out.
Getting easily distracted, careless mistakes and losing things.
Forgetfulness, difficulty organizing and procrastinating.
All of the key signs of inattentive presenting ADHD.
Interrupting others, blurting out answers without recognition and difficulty waiting in line.
Short temper and impatience, which with therapy have gotten better, thankfully.
Fidgeting, inability to sit still, feeling internally restless and EXCESSIVE talking.
All symptoms of ADHD with hyperactive presentation.
HAPPY MID-DUMP GENGAR!
I GOT'EM ALL! WOO!
Honestly, though, it does help to ease my mind and forgive myself.
I used to blame myself for all of the symptoms above, thinking I just needed to be better.
However, I had actually tried to work on every issue as an individual problem.
Instead of the megazord of symptoms that they are.
Now, I don't want this to feel like a crutch of a diagnosis and lean into it.
I want to do all of the things, i.e. therapy, medicine and other treatments, to help overcome it.
I've always felt like I've had the answers to a lot of questions, in life and school.
But, I understand the importance of asking for assistance and how it isn't a weakness.
The other reason it feels so important is, now I don't feel as guilty as I used to do.
I would procrastinate in school on projects and at work with deadlines.
I thought I was just lazy and, no matter how hard I tried, I couldn't motivate myself until the pressure.
I lose things like it's my superpower, regardless of if I throw it somewhere or purposely place it down.
I despise waiting so much, I will drive an extra 3 minutes to not sit in traffic.
I can't help but feel the urge to interrupt others during a story or conversation.
I am constantly talking to anyone or anything, including myself and inanimate objects.
I have trouble with 2 hour movies or 40 minute long TV shows, but will game for literally hours on end.
I make purchases with little thought or I convince myself I need it. (Again gotten better with age, kinda)
All of that is to say, the signs have been there looking back and I wish I could've found out earlier.
BUT, now my journey begins to try and get this under control so I can be my best self.
Not just for myself, but my wonderful wife and beautiful daughter.
Thanks for reading my story and taking in the stolen memes that have given me great pleasure recently!
I'm this picture and I don't like it.
So, have a wonderful time and seek out help if you feel like you need it!
XOXOXO Willste
item.dc:creator: Willste
